words.mean_en:
1. weighing a lot; difficult to lift or move: I can't carry this bag _ it's too heavy (OPPOSITE: light)
2. larger, stronger or more than usual: heavy rain - the traffic was very heavy this morning (OPPOSITE: light)
looking or listening carefully and with interest: Can I have your attention, please? (= please listen to me)
to look or listen carefully: Please pay attention to what I'm saying
something that you must talk about or do: There is a matter I would like to discuss with you
a person who is between 13 and 19 years old
realy: Do you think he's genuinely sorry?
to be good or bad enough to have somethong: You have worked very hard and you deserve a rest - They stole money from old people, so they deserve to go to prison
to have clothes, jewellery, etc. on your body: She was wearing a red dress - I wear glasses

1. a word that you use to say that something is surprising: The game is so easy that even a child can play it - He didn't laugh _ he didn't even smile
2. a word that you use to make another word stronger: Their house is even smaller than ours
